title: Math 314 Final Practice
description: Linear algebra with full context and formula-focused answers.

questions.prompt: Given vectors $$u= egin{bmatrix}1\-2\1\end{bmatrix}, v= egin{bmatrix}0\1\2\end{bmatrix}, w= egin{bmatrix}-5\-2\1\end{bmatrix}$$. Orthogonality rule: $$u \cdot v = 0$$. Check all pairwise dot products.
questions.explanation: All dot products equal 0 so vectors are orthogonal.
All dot products equal 0 so vectors are orthogonal.

questions.prompt: Independence rule: orthogonal nonzero vectors are independent. Using results from dot products above.
questions.explanation: All vectors are nonzero and orthogonal.

questions.prompt: Span rule in R^3: 3 independent vectors span R^3.
questions.explanation: Dimension equals number of independent vectors.

questions.prompt: Basis rule: basis = independent + span.
Given matrix $$A=\begin{bmatrix}1&0&-5\\-2&1&0\\1&2&5\end{bmatrix}$$. Pivot rule: independent = number of pivots equals number of columns.
Rank-nullity formula: rank = total columns - nullity. Given 7x6 matrix with nullity 4.
Orthogonal complement rule: dim(W^perp) = n - dim(W). Given W subset R^5 with dim 2.
Dependence rule: number of vectors > dimension implies dependence. For 4 vectors in R^3.
Injective rule: injective if null(A) = 0.
Diagonalization rule: diagonalizable if number of independent eigenvectors equals n.
Invertibility rule: invertible if pivots = n.
Diagonalization inverse formula: A^{-1} = P D^{-1} P^{-1}.
Eigenvalue power rule: A^2 squares eigenvalues.
Determinant rule: det(A) = product of eigenvalues.
Vector space rule: Ax = 0 defines a subspace.
Rank-nullity: rank + nullity = n for 3x3 matrix with rank 2.
Transpose null relation: dim(null(A^T)) = m - rank(A).
Repeated eigenvalues may fail diagonalization.
Distinct eigenvalues guarantee independence.
Rotation matrix eigenvalues: lambda = cos(theta) ± i sin(theta). Real only when?
Symmetric matrix definition.
